result.summary: Lyft exposes one documented HTTP API at `https://api.lyft.com/v1`, covering public, user, sandbox, and restricted endpoints, authenticated with OAuth 2.0 via a Lyft developer app.
result.description: Lyft is a transportation platform for on-demand rides and business ride programs. Its developer offering includes API access for ride-related integrations, including Lyft Business Concierge use cases.

lyft_oauth_app.label: Lyft developer app (Client ID and Client Secret)
lyft_oauth_app.setup: Sign in to the [Lyft Developer Portal](https://www.lyft.com/developers), create an app/client, and use the credentials shown on the app's credentials page. For Lyft Business use cases, connect that client to a program in the [Lyft Business Portal](https://business.lyft.com/login) and approve the connection from the app's `Programs` tab as described in [Managing your API client and program connections](https://help.lyft.com/business/hc/en-us/articles/8587470351891-Managing-your-API-client-and-program-connections). The OpenAPI spec indicates tokens are minted from `https://api.lyft.com/oauth/token`, with app-only access for the `public` scope and user-delegated access available via OAuth authorize/token endpoints.
